issues.title: When I specify the default Advisor myself, if I don't explicitly specify the default Advisor for the framework again, I will report an error when calling ChatClient.

comments.body:
Actually, this is because you're trying to use `ChatClient.CallResponseSpec` twice.
```java
ChatClient.CallResponseSpec response = teacherModelClient.
                prompt()
                .system(sp -> sp.param("voice", "cat"))
                .user("Hello")
                .call();
        ChatResponse chatResponse = response.chatResponse();  // first
        System.out.println(chatResponse);
        String voice1 = response.content(); // second
        System.out.println(voice1);
```
The `Advisors` list gets used up during the first call, so it's empty when you try to use it again the second time.

> Actually, this is because you're trying to use `ChatClient.CallResponseSpec` twice.
> ```java
> ChatClient.CallResponseSpec response = teacherModelClient.
>                 prompt()
>                 .system(sp -> sp.param("voice", "cat"))
>                 .user("Hello")
>                 .call();
>         ChatResponse chatResponse = response.chatResponse();  // first
>         System.out.println(chatResponse);
>         String voice1 = response.content(); // second
>         System.out.println(voice1);
> ```
> The `Advisors` list gets used up during the first call, so it's empty when you try to use it again the second time.

ok,got it,thanks

issues.title: The Bedrock Converse document needs to be updated
issues.body:
Since #3770 has already been merged, we need to synchronously update the relevant section in the documentation: 

https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/blob/9d1e1b5b152ee330e1f7a09e59391ea007871442/spring-ai-docs/src/main/antora/modules/ROOT/pages/api/chat/bedrock-converse.adoc?plain=1#L109-L121

I'm sorry I missed this in that PR. I will submit a PR to fix the documentation.
